'''Bailey Hodgson''' (born 5 September 2002) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] and [[Blacktown Workers]] in the [[NSW Cup]].

==Background==
Hodgson was born in [[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]], [[England]].He is the nephew of [[Parramatta Eels]] and [[England national rugby league team|English]] international {{rlp|hk}} [[Josh Hodgson]].<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2020/11/19/bailey-hodgson-joins-knights-for-21-season/|title=Bailey Hodgson joins Knights for '21 season|date=November 19, 2020|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Castleford Tigers===
In 2020, Hodgson made his [[Super League]] début for [[Castleford Tigers]] in round 17 against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP3214924|title=Super League: Castleford Tigers v Hull KR|publisher=BBC}}</ref>

===Newcastle Knights===
In November 2020, Hodgson signed a three-year contract with [[Australia]]n side [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]] starting in 2021.<ref name="auto"/>

After three injury riddled years at the Newcastle outfit, Hodgson parted ways with the club at the end of the 2023 season, failing to play an NRL match.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2023/09/05/kalyn-ponga-claims-2023-danny-buderus-medal/|title=Kalyn Ponga claims 2023 Danny Buderus Medal|date=5 September 2023|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref>

===Manly Sea Eagles & Blacktown Workers===
On 6 November, it was reported that he had signed with the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]], on the supplementary list.<ref>{{cite web|website=Hull Daily Mail|date=6 Nov 2023|title=Hull-born Bailey Hodgson lands NRL lifeline after leaving Newcastle Knights|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-born-bailey-hodgson-lands-8884685}}</ref>

He also appeared in pre-season ahead of the current campaign for Manly, but again failed to make a competitive senior appearance this year for the Sea Eagles.

Instead, he has featured for their feeder club – the [[Blacktown Workers]] – in the New South Wales Cup.

===Leigh Leopards===
On 25 July 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the [[Super League]] on a 2½-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 Jul 2024|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Bailey Hodgson: The lowdown on Leigh Leopards’ new recruit including possible position, squad role|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bailey-hodgson-the-lowdown-on-leigh-leopards-new-recruit-including-possible-position-squad-role}}</ref>
